title: Mischophloeus paniculatus
intro: Mischophloeus paniculatus, also known as Paniculata or Indian Pennywort, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mischophloeus paniculatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mischophloeus paniculatus is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in the form of capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Mischophloeus paniculatus is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree, typically growing up to 10 meters in height. Mischophloeus paniculatus is found in various types of habitats, including forests and woodlands. Its classification places it within the plant kingdom, specifically in the class Magnoliopsida and the order Magnoliales.
